asset_guidance.production_asset_note: Any custom icons, illustrations, or imagery should be tracked as explicit production assets before implementation.
asset_guidance.source_of_truth: The canonical contract defines the production design. Inspiration assets are references only.

component_guidance.button.primary: Keep as the singular primary action treatment on a screen. Maintain clear spacing around it.
component_guidance.card.default: Use for modular grouped content. Keep shape, surface, and padding aligned with the contract rather than native defaults alone.

principles: Push reusable visual rules into shared styles or modifiers.
Keep semantic roles clearer than view-level styling tricks.
design_intent.summary: Implement the validated mobile contract in SwiftUI while keeping typography, hierarchy, and interaction tone aligned with the canonical design.

implementation_notes: Prefer shared modifiers and style wrappers over screen-local styling drift.
Keep accessibility and dynamic type equal in priority to visual fidelity.

interaction_guidance.disabled.opacity: Disabled states should communicate unavailable actions without losing readability.
interaction_guidance.pressed.scale: Pressed feedback should stay subtle and fast. Keep it supportive, not theatrical.

layout_guidance.scroll.vertical: Assume a vertical mobile flow and keep early content focused.

typography_guidance.app.body: Use for primary explanatory copy and content text.
typography_guidance.app.display: Reserve for hero moments such as onboarding and first-run steps. Keep scaling behavior accessible.
typography_guidance.app.title: Use for page-level hierarchy and section headlines. Avoid introducing alternate title styles without contract changes.

visual_guidance.action.primary: Use as the dominant interactive accent for the main CTA and action-led components.
visual_guidance.surface.canvas: Use as the baseline background surface for each screen.
visual_guidance.surface.card: Use for grouped modules needing elevation or separation without crowding the canvas.
visual_guidance.text.onAction: Use for text sitting on top of the primary action color and validate contrast in all states.
